mission.title: Install and Configure Oxia on Kubernetes
mission.description: Oxia is a robust and scalable metadata store and coordination system designed for large-scale distributed systems. Installing Oxia allows you to efficiently manage metadata in cloud-native applications, ensuring scalability and high availability.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Oxia to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy Oxia Components
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Oxia components.

steps.title: Verify Deployment
steps.description: Check the status of the pods to ensure that Oxia is running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Oxia will show the Oxia pods running in the 'oxia' namespace. You will also be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine.

uninstall.title: Remove Oxia Deployment
uninstall.description: Delete the Oxia resources and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Oxia
upgrade.description: To upgrade Oxia, apply the updated manifests from the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the Oxia pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If the service is not accessible, ensure that the service type is set correctly (e.g., LoadBalancer or NodePort).

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are in a pending state, check if there are sufficient resources available in the cluster.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
